input.prompt: Add a support ticket classifier to this .NET 10 Web API. Tickets have a Subject (string), Description (string), Priority (int 1-3), and Category should be predicted as one of: Billing, Technical, General. Use the provided training data CSV. Show me the full implementation including model training, evaluation metrics, and a prediction endpoint.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the project. Let me build the full ML.NET-based ticket classifier. I need to:

1. Add ML.NET NuGet package
2. Create models (TicketData, TicketPrediction)
3. Create a service that trains the model, evaluates it, and makes predictions
4. Create a controller with endpoints for training/evaluation and prediction
5. Update Program.cs to wire everything up

Let me plan the file structure:
- Models/TicketData.cs - Input model
- Models/TicketPrediction.cs - Output model


- Services/TicketClassifierService.cs - ML training and prediction
- Controllers/TicketClassifierController.cs - API endpoints
- Update Program.cs and .csproj for ML.NET dependencies

Now I'll start building all these components.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "sleep 5 && curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5199/api/ticket-classifier/train | python3 -m json.tool", "description": "Train the model", "initial_wait": 30}

arguments.command:
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5199/api/ticket-classifier/predict \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"subject":"Overcharged","description":"I was billed $50 instead of $25","priority":2}' | python3 -m json.tool && \
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5199/api/ticket-classifier/predict \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"subject":"App crash","description":"The application crashes on startup with a null reference","priority":1}' | python3 -m json.tool && \
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5199/api/ticket-classifier/predict \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"subject":"Question","description":"How do I enable two-factor authentication?","priority":3}' | python3 -m json.tool

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5199/api/ticket-classifier/predict \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"subject":"Overcharged","description":"I was billed $50 instead of $25","priority":2}' | python3 -m json.tool && \
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5199/api/ticket-classifier/predict \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"subject":"App crash","description":"The application crashes on startup with a null reference","priority":1}' | python3 -m json.tool && \
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5199/api/ticket-classifier/predict \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \

data.content:
Everything works. Here's what was built:

**Files created/modified:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `TicketClassifier.csproj` | Added `Microsoft.ML 4.0.2` |
| `Models/TicketData.cs` | ML.NET input/output data classes with `[LoadColumn]` attributes |
| `Models/TicketRequest.cs` | API DTOs: request, prediction response, evaluation metrics |
| `Services/TicketClassifierService.cs` | ML pipeline — text featurization of Subject+Description, Priority as numeric feature, SDCA Maximum Entropy multiclass trainer, 3-fold cross-validation |
| `Controllers/TicketClassifierController.cs` | Two endpoints below |
| `Program.cs` | Registered the service as singleton |

**Endpoints:**
- **`POST /api/ticket-classifier/train`** — Trains on `Data/tickets.csv`, returns accuracy, log-loss, and per-class precision/recall/F1
- **`POST /api/ticket-classifier/predict`** — Accepts `{subject, description, priority}`, returns predicted category + per-class probabilities
